name: Sickle Cell Disease
summary: Sickle cell disease is a monogenic hemoglobinopathy caused by pathogenic HBB variants; population-level outcomes depend on care access and exposure context. GENARCH provides educational summaries only.
adolescent_relevance: Often manifests in childhood/adolescence; GENARCH provides educational mechanism context only.

genetic_architecture.prs_notes: PRS not applicable as primary model for monogenic SCD.

exposure_modifiers.mechanism_hypothesis: Infections can increase inflammatory stress in population contexts.
Heat/dehydration are physiological stressors discussed in cohorts; context-dependent.
Respiratory stressor context; evidence varies by setting.

population_equity.gwas_ancestry_breakdown: SCD prevalence varies by ancestry due to historical selection; care access differs across settings.
population_equity.transferability_notes: Outcome studies may not generalize across contexts due to differences in care and exposure.
population_equity.data_gaps: Environmental exposure linkages to outcomes are limited and setting-specific.

references.title: Sickle cell disease: molecular basis and complications (review)
Infection exposure and immune-mediated disease mechanisms: overview (review)
Extreme heat exposure and health outcomes: overview (review)
Air pollution and health: evidence and mechanisms (review)
references.journal: Journal of the Air & Waste Management Association
